symptoms.symptom_name: Two episodes today of presyncope on mild exertion/walking, recurring on standing, each easing with rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block, possible progression toward higher-degree AV block)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New recurrent exertional presyncope in an older adult is a classic feature of an intermittent bradyarrhythmia or high-degree conduction block, particularly concerning when new/recent onset.
Recurrent exertional presyncope without a clear vasovagal trigger (pain, emotional stress, prolonged standing) is atypical for simple vasovagal syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild, non-radiating chest heaviness accompanying the presyncope and fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest heaviness in an older hypertensive patient with exertional symptoms raises concern for concurrent myocardial ischaemia, even though described as mild and non-radiating.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block, possible progression toward higher-degree AV block)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest heaviness accompanying presyncope raises concern that an ischaemic process may be driving new or worsening conduction disease, which is an important mechanistic link in this age group.

symptoms.symptom_name: Background generalised fatigue over the past month, culminating today in first episodes of presyncope

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block, possible progression toward higher-degree AV block)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A progressive timeline of fatigue culminating in acute presyncope is consistent with an evolving conduction or rhythm disturbance rather than a stable incidental finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional shortness of breath accompanying the dizzy spells and chest heaviness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block, possible progression toward higher-degree AV block)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional dyspnoea accompanying presyncope and chest heaviness is consistent with reduced cardiac output from a bradyarrhythmia or intermittent conduction block, or with concurrent cardiac ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No palpitations and no actual loss of consciousness (only near-fainting)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block, possible progression toward higher-degree AV block)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of palpitations and frank syncope does not exclude an intermittent conduction block, which can present with presyncope alone; this pertinent negative narrows but does not exclude the differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widely and fixed-split second heart sound (S2) on auscultation, not varying with respiration

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Wide, fixed splitting of S2 is a classic auscultatory sign of significant intraventricular conduction delay (e.g. right bundle branch block), distinguishing it from normal physiological respiratory variation in S2 splitting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widened QRS complex (>120ms) with rsR' morphology in V1 (RBBB) or broad monophasic R waves in lateral leads (LBBB) on 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: QRS duration >120ms with either rsR' pattern in V1 (right bundle branch block) or broad monophasic R waves in lateral leads I, aVL, V5-V6 (left bundle branch block), with assessment for any ST/T changes suggesting concurrent ischaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Bundle branch block cannot be confirmed or typed, and any evolving higher-degree AV block or ischaemic change would be missed, delaying appropriate monitoring and treatment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The widened QRS with characteristic morphology is the defining ECG diagnostic criterion for bundle branch block.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Symptomatic conduction system disease (bundle branch block, possible progression toward higher-degree AV block)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of new recurrent exertional presyncope, mild chest heaviness, a month-long prodrome of fatigue, and exertional dyspnoea in a 74-year-old with hypertension together form a high-concern constellation for new or progressing conduction system disease, possibly ischaemia-driven, requiring urgent ECG and monitoring regardless of the exact final rhythm diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory first-line investigation to characterise the QRS duration/morphology, confirm the bundle branch block type, and screen for concurrent ischaemic changes or evolving higher-degree AV block. Cannot be performed via telehealth — requires emergency department or monitored setting.
Required to assess for concurrent myocardial ischaemia as a possible driver of new conduction disease, given the chest heaviness and risk profile.
Required to detect any evolving higher-degree AV block or bradyarrhythmia while the patient remains symptomatic; only available in a monitored inpatient/ED setting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient Holter monitor as the sole initial investigation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not appropriate as the first-line response to acute presyncope with chest heaviness — this requires urgent in-person assessment and monitoring, not a delayed outpatient study.
